logs.message: %c%s%c Using the user object as returned from supabase.auth.getSession() or from some supabase.auth.onAuthStateChange() events could be insecure! This value comes directly from the storage medium (usually cookies on the server) and may not be authentic. Use supabase.auth.getUser() instead which authenticates the data by contacting the Supabase Auth server. background: #e6e6e6;background: light-dark(rgba(0,0,0,0.1), rgba(255,255,255,0.25));color: #000000;color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff);border-radius: 2px  Server

A tree hydrated but some attributes of the server rendered HTML didn't match the client properties. This won't be patched up. This can happen if a SSR-ed Client Component used:

- A server/client branch `if (typeof window !== 'undefined')`.
- Variable input such as `Date.now()` or `Math.random()` which changes each time it's called.
- Date formatting in a user's locale which doesn't match the server.
- External changing data without sending a snapshot of it along with the HTML.
- Invalid HTML tag nesting.

It can also happen if the client has a browser extension installed which messes with the HTML before React loaded.

%s%s https://react.dev/link/hydration-mismatch 

  ...
    <Popper __scopePopper={{Popper:[...]}}>
      <PopperProvider scope={{Popper:[...]}} anchor={null} onAnchorChange={function bound dispatchSetState}>
        <PopoverProvider scope={undefined} contentId="radix-_R_3..." triggerRef={{current:null}} open={false} ...>
          <PopoverTrigger asChild={true}>
            <PopoverTrigger data-slot="popover-tr..." asChild={true}>
              <PopperAnchor asChild={true} __scopePopper={{Popper:[...]}}>
                <Primitive.div asChild={true} ref={function}>
                  <Primitive.div.Slot ref={function}>
                    <Primitive.div.SlotClone ref={function}>
                      <Primitive.button type="button" aria-haspopup="dialog" aria-expanded={false} ...>
                        <Primitive.button.Slot type="button" aria-haspopup="dialog" aria-expanded={false} ...>
                          <Primitive.button.SlotClone type="button" aria-haspopup="dialog" aria-expanded={false} ...>
                            <Button aria-label="Claude Hai..." className="max-w-full..." disabled={false} size="sm" ...>
                              <button
                                data-slot="popover-trigger"
                                data-variant="ghost"
                                data-size="sm"
                                className={"group/button inline-flex shrink-0 items-center border border-transparent ..."}
                                aria-label="Claude Haiku 4.5"
                                disabled={false}
                                type="button"
                                aria-haspopup="dialog"
                                aria-expanded={false}
+                               aria-controls="radix-_R_3qeanebnabmlb_"
-                               aria-controls="radix-_R_f9patpetabmlb_"
                                data-state="closed"
                                onClick={function handleEvent}
                                ref={function}
                              >
          ...

This value comes directly from the storage medium (usually cookies on the server) and may not be authentic. Use supabase.auth.getUser() instead which authenticates the data by contacting the Supabase Auth server. background: #e6e6e6;background: light-dark(rgba(0,0,0,0.1), rgba(255,255,255,0.25));color: #000000;color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff);border-radius: 2px  Server

A tree hydrated but some attributes of the server rendered HTML didn't match the client properties. This won't be patched up. This can happen if a SSR-ed Client Component used:

- A server/client branch `if (typeof window !== 'undefined')`.
- Variable input such as `Date.now()` or `Math.random()` which changes each time it's called.
- Date formatting in a user's locale which doesn't match the server.
- External changing data without sending a snapshot of it along with the HTML.
- Invalid HTML tag nesting.

It can also happen if the client has a browser extension installed which messes with the HTML before React loaded.

logs.message: %c%s%c Using the user object as returned from supabase.auth.getSession() or from some supabase.auth.onAuthStateChange() events could be insecure! This value comes directly from the storage medium (usually cookies on the server) and may not be authentic. Use supabase.auth.getUser() instead which authenticates the data by contacting the Supabase Auth server. background: #e6e6e6;background: light-dark(rgba(0,0,0,0.1), rgba(255,255,255,0.25));color: #000000;color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff);border-radius: 2px  Server

A tree hydrated but some attributes of the server rendered HTML didn't match the client properties. This won't be patched up. This can happen if a SSR-ed Client Component used:

- A server/client branch `if (typeof window !== 'undefined')`.
- Variable input such as `Date.now()` or `Math.random()` which changes each time it's called.
- Date formatting in a user's locale which doesn't match the server.
- External changing data without sending a snapshot of it along with the HTML.
- Invalid HTML tag nesting.

It can also happen if the client has a browser extension installed which messes with the HTML before React loaded.

logs.message: %c%s%c Using the user object as returned from supabase.auth.getSession() or from some supabase.auth.onAuthStateChange() events could be insecure! This value comes directly from the storage medium (usually cookies on the server) and may not be authentic. Use supabase.auth.getUser() instead which authenticates the data by contacting the Supabase Auth server. background: #e6e6e6;background: light-dark(rgba(0,0,0,0.1), rgba(255,255,255,0.25));color: #000000;color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff);border-radius: 2px  Server

A tree hydrated but some attributes of the server rendered HTML didn't match the client properties. This won't be patched up. This can happen if a SSR-ed Client Component used:

- A server/client branch `if (typeof window !== 'undefined')`.
- Variable input such as `Date.now()` or `Math.random()` which changes each time it's called.
- Date formatting in a user's locale which doesn't match the server.
- External changing data without sending a snapshot of it along with the HTML.
- Invalid HTML tag nesting.

It can also happen if the client has a browser extension installed which messes with the HTML before React loaded.

Use supabase.auth.getUser() instead which authenticates the data by contacting the Supabase Auth server. background: #e6e6e6;background: light-dark(rgba(0,0,0,0.1), rgba(255,255,255,0.25));color: #000000;color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff);border-radius: 2px  Server

A tree hydrated but some attributes of the server rendered HTML didn't match the client properties. This won't be patched up. This can happen if a SSR-ed Client Component used:

- A server/client branch `if (typeof window !== 'undefined')`.
- Variable input such as `Date.now()` or `Math.random()` which changes each time it's called.
- Date formatting in a user's locale which doesn't match the server.
- External changing data without sending a snapshot of it along with the HTML.
- Invalid HTML tag nesting.

It can also happen if the client has a browser extension installed which messes with the HTML before React loaded.

A tree hydrated but some attributes of the server rendered HTML didn't match the client properties. This won't be patched up. This can happen if a SSR-ed Client Component used:

- A server/client branch `if (typeof window !== 'undefined')`.
- Variable input such as `Date.now()` or `Math.random()` which changes each time it's called.
- Date formatting in a user's locale which doesn't match the server.
- External changing data without sending a snapshot of it along with the HTML.
- Invalid HTML tag nesting.

It can also happen if the client has a browser extension installed which messes with the HTML before React loaded.

A tree hydrated but some attributes of the server rendered HTML didn't match the client properties. This won't be patched up. This can happen if a SSR-ed Client Component used:

- A server/client branch `if (typeof window !== 'undefined')`.
- Variable input such as `Date.now()` or `Math.random()` which changes each time it's called.
- Date formatting in a user's locale which doesn't match the server.
- External changing data without sending a snapshot of it along with the HTML.
- Invalid HTML tag nesting.

It can also happen if the client has a browser extension installed which messes with the HTML before React loaded.

This value comes directly from the storage medium (usually cookies on the server) and may not be authentic. Use supabase.auth.getUser() instead which authenticates the data by contacting the Supabase Auth server. background: #e6e6e6;background: light-dark(rgba(0,0,0,0.1), rgba(255,255,255,0.25));color: #000000;color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff);border-radius: 2px  Server

A tree hydrated but some attributes of the server rendered HTML didn't match the client properties. This won't be patched up. This can happen if a SSR-ed Client Component used:

- A server/client branch `if (typeof window !== 'undefined')`.
- Variable input such as `Date.now()` or `Math.random()` which changes each time it's called.
- Date formatting in a user's locale which doesn't match the server.
- External changing data without sending a snapshot of it along with the HTML.
- Invalid HTML tag nesting.

It can also happen if the client has a browser extension installed which messes with the HTML before React loaded.

- A server/client branch `if (typeof window !== 'undefined')`.

- Date formatting in a user's locale which doesn't match the server.

Use supabase.auth.getUser() instead which authenticates the data by contacting the Supabase Auth server. background: #e6e6e6;background: light-dark(rgba(0,0,0,0.1), rgba(255,255,255,0.25));color: #000000;color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff);border-radius: 2px  Server

A tree hydrated but some attributes of the server rendered HTML didn't match the client properties. This won't be patched up. This can happen if a SSR-ed Client Component used:

- A server/client branch `if (typeof window !== 'undefined')`.
- Variable input such as `Date.now()` or `Math.random()` which changes each time it's called.
- Date formatting in a user's locale which doesn't match the server.
- External changing data without sending a snapshot of it along with the HTML.
- Invalid HTML tag nesting.

It can also happen if the client has a browser extension installed which messes with the HTML before React loaded.

Use supabase.auth.getUser() instead which authenticates the data by contacting the Supabase Auth server. background: #e6e6e6;background: light-dark(rgba(0,0,0,0.1), rgba(255,255,255,0.25));color: #000000;color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff);border-radius: 2px  Server

A tree hydrated but some attributes of the server rendered HTML didn't match the client properties. This won't be patched up. This can happen if a SSR-ed Client Component used:

- A server/client branch `if (typeof window !== 'undefined')`.
- Variable input such as `Date.now()` or `Math.random()` which changes each time it's called.
- Date formatting in a user's locale which doesn't match the server.
- External changing data without sending a snapshot of it along with the HTML.
- Invalid HTML tag nesting.

It can also happen if the client has a browser extension installed which messes with the HTML before React loaded.

Use supabase.auth.getUser() instead which authenticates the data by contacting the Supabase Auth server. background: #e6e6e6;background: light-dark(rgba(0,0,0,0.1), rgba(255,255,255,0.25));color: #000000;color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff);border-radius: 2px  Server

A tree hydrated but some attributes of the server rendered HTML didn't match the client properties. This won't be patched up. This can happen if a SSR-ed Client Component used:

- A server/client branch `if (typeof window !== 'undefined')`.
- Variable input such as `Date.now()` or `Math.random()` which changes each time it's called.
- Date formatting in a user's locale which doesn't match the server.
- External changing data without sending a snapshot of it along with the HTML.
- Invalid HTML tag nesting.

It can also happen if the client has a browser extension installed which messes with the HTML before React loaded.

logs.message: %c%s%c Using the user object as returned from supabase.auth.getSession() or from some supabase.auth.onAuthStateChange() events could be insecure! This value comes directly from the storage medium (usually cookies on the server) and may not be authentic. Use supabase.auth.getUser() instead which authenticates the data by contacting the Supabase Auth server. background: #e6e6e6;background: light-dark(rgba(0,0,0,0.1), rgba(255,255,255,0.25));color: #000000;color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff);border-radius: 2px  Server

A tree hydrated but some attributes of the server rendered HTML didn't match the client properties. This won't be patched up. This can happen if a SSR-ed Client Component used:

- A server/client branch `if (typeof window !== 'undefined')`.
- Variable input such as `Date.now()` or `Math.random()` which changes each time it's called.
- Date formatting in a user's locale which doesn't match the server.
- External changing data without sending a snapshot of it along with the HTML.
- Invalid HTML tag nesting.

It can also happen if the client has a browser extension installed which messes with the HTML before React loaded.
